title: Roasted Flaky Spanish Beans Pa amb Tomaquet
description: A hearty and comforting Spanish brunch dish featuring roasted pinto beans, crispy bread, and a sweet and tangy tomato topping. Perfect for a lazy morning or afternoon.

ingredients.name: Extra Virgin Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality, cold-pressed oil for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Minced or crushed, whichever suits your preference.

ingredients.name: Smoked Paprika
ingredients.notes: Adds a deep, smoky flavor to the beans.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt or sea salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Yakimono-Grill Bread
ingredients.notes: A crusty bread with a chewy interior, perfect for sopping up the tomato sauce.

ingredients.name: Fresh Tomato
ingredients.notes: Heirloom or beefsteak, whichever you prefer.

instructions: Drizzle the olive oil over the pinto beans and toss to coat.
Spread the beans in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes, or until lightly browned.
While the beans are roasting, toast the bread slices until crispy and golden brown.
In a small bowl, mix together the minced garlic, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Once the bread is toasted, brush it with the additional olive oil and rub with the garlic-paprika mixture.
To assemble the pa amb tomaquet, place a toasted bread slice on a plate or board.
Top the bread with a spoonful of the roasted beans, a slice or two of fresh tomato, and a sprinkle of red pepper flakes (if using).
Drizzle the tomato with a little extra olive oil and serve immediately.
